evidences.content: For sharp bettors and prop research, implied probability is the single most important number on the screen. It tells you what the market believes — and gives you a baseline to compare against your own model, projections, or AI confidence score. If you believe a player’s true probability of going Over is 62% and the book’s implied probability is only 56%, you have a 6% edge — that’s the foundation of positive expected value (+EV) betting. ## How to calculate implied probability by hand ### From American odds Negative odds (favorites, e.g. `-150`): `|odds| / (|odds| + 100) × 100` → 150 / 250 × 100 = 60.00% Positive odds (underdogs, e.g. `+200`): `100 / (odds + 100) × 100` → 100 / 300 × 100 = 33.33% ### From decimal odds [...] ## What is implied probability in sports betting? Implied probability is the win-rate baked into a sportsbook’s price for a bet. Every set of odds — whether -110, +200, or 2.50 — translates to a percentage representing how often that outcome must occur for the bet to be a long-term break-even. evidences.title: La Liga | History | Research Starters - EBSCO
evidences.content: The new league formed two divisions. The top league, Campeonato Nacional de Liga de Primera, or La Liga, was made up of ten teams. Six were previous winners of the Copa del Rey, the national championship then known as the Copa del Ayuntamiento de Madrid, or Madrid City Council’s Cup: Arenas Club de Getxo, Athletic Bilbao, Barcelona, Real Madrid, Real Sociedad, and Real Unión.
